MINI-MINUTES FOR MAR-05-2004 9:30 AM FOR DEPT 301

LAW AND MOTION, 301, WHEN THE CASE WAS CALLED, NO APPEARANCE FOR PLAINTIFF KELLEY AND DEFENDANT SUNIGA. COUNSEL FOR DEFENDANT GREGORY ARMIJO SUBMITTED TO TENTATIVE RULING. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING: DEFENDANT GREGORY ARMIJO'S AMENDED MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS: DENIED; DEFENDANT WAIVED HIS RIGHT TO CONTEST PERSONAL JURISDICTION WHEN HE FILED HIS PREJUDGMENT CLAIM OF RIGHT TO POSSESSION. THE COURT ORDERED COUNSEL TO SUBMIT AN ORDER PURSUANT TO CCP 391. COUNSEL SCHIVO AND DROHAN APPEARED AFTER THE HEARING. ON THE RECORD, THE COURT ORDERED COUNSEL TO MEET AND CONFER AND AGREE TO A FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: KEVIN M. MCCARTHY REPORTER: CHRISTINA PAXTON, CSR #1558

Off Calendar Mar-22-2004 Off Calendar

NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. DEFENDANT GREGORY ARMIJO'S Motion To Quash Service Of Summons: OFF CALENDAR; matter heard on 03/05/04. (301/REQ/) Reporter: Beldon Yee, CSR #2647

AMENDED MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
Off Calendar Mar-05-2004 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Nancy Lawlor, Esq. of Dimitriou & Associates 434-1144, for defendant Gregory Armijo, moving to quash service fo summons
Ronald D. Schivo, Esq. of LAW OFFICES OF ANDREW M. ZACKS 956-8100, for plaintiff and opposing party Karry Kelley
Thomas E. Drohan, Esq. of LEGAL ASSISTANCE TO THE ELDERLY, INC. 861-4444, for defendant Esther Suniga supporting the motion to quash

RULING:
When the case was called, no appearance for plaintiff Kelley and defendant Suniga. Counsel for defendant Gregory Armijo submitted to tentative ruling. The Court adopted its tentative ruling: DEFENDANT GREGORY ARMIJO'S AMENDED MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS: Denied; defendant waived his right to contest personal jurisdiction when he filed his prejudgment claim of right to possession. The Court ordered counsel to submit an order pursuant to CCP 391.
Counsel Schivo and Drohan appeared after the hearing. On the record, the Court ordered counsel to meet and confer and agree to a form of order. (301/KMM/EH) Reporter: Christina Paxton, CSR #1558

Notice Of Motion To Quash Service Of Summons
Off Calendar Feb-20-2004 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Thomas E. Drohan, Esq., Legal Assistance to the Elderly, Inc. (415) 861-4444, for Defendant and Moving Party Suniga
Ronald D. Schivo, Esq., Law Offices of Andrew Zacks (4150 956-1800, for Plaintiff and Opposing Party Kelley
RULING:
A R G U E D; the Court adopted its Tentative Ruling: DEFENDANT ESTHER SUNIGA'S Motion To Quash Service Of Summons: DENIED with 5 days leave to answer. Prevailing party to submit form of order. (301/JLW/EH) Reporter: Christina Paxton, CSR #1558
